data.title: Wholesale inventories fell 0.5% M/M in January
data.description:
Advance wholesale inventories (January): -0.5% M/M to $909.3B vs. +0.2% consensus and -0.1% to $914.4B prior (revised), according to data released by the U.S. Census Bureau on Thursday.

Developing... Check back for updates.

data.title: Trump, Takaichi to announce $40B GE Vernova Hitachi reactor project
data.description:
President Trump and Japanese Prime Minister Takaichi are set to announce a $40B nuclear power project in Tennessee, where GE Vernova (GEV) and Hitachi (HTHIY) (HTHIF) will build BWRX-300 small modular nuclear reactors, Bloomberg reported Thursday; specific details, including when the reactors would be operational, are not yet clear.

The Trump administration believes small modular nuclear reactors would help boost U.S. industrial growth by adding power that can be generated on demand.

According to previous reports, the Trump administration was unable to reach a nuclear project deal with Westinghouse owner Brookfield Asset Management (BAM) and Cameco (CCJ).

The reactor deal is the latest from the $550B fund that the U.S. and Japan have planned as part of an agreement that included reduced auto tariffs and other levies; the two countries also unveiled three projects totaling $36B last month, including a U.S. oil export terminal, gas power plant, and synthetic diamond manufacturing facility.

data.title: Silver tumbles into bear market as $70 breaks, ‘rug pull’ fears grow
data.description:
Silver slid below $70, extending a sharp downturn that has pushed the metal into bear market territory after a steep reversal from early-March highs.

Prices are now down nearly 30% since March 2, with losses accelerating as the iShares Silver Trust (SLV) broke below its 100-day moving average, a key technical support level that traders say has triggered systematic selling.

Market focus has also turned to shifts in price-setting dynamics.

“Warned a month ago that Jane Street becoming the biggest marginal price setter guarantees a rug pull,” Zerohedge said in a post on X, highlighting concerns that liquidity dominated by high-frequency trading firms can amplify reversals once momentum fades.

Analysts said the speed of the decline points more to positioning being unwound than a deterioration in underlying fundamentals. With liquidity thinning and volatility rising, the breakdown in technical levels suggests further downside risk unless prices stabilize quickly.

The latest move underscores how flow-driven rallies can reverse abruptly when key participants step back, leaving markets vulnerable to sharp air-pocket declines.

data.title: Hilton adds YOTEL properties to 'Select by Hilton' platform
data.description:
As part of its asset-light model, Hilton (HLT) has added independently owned YOTEL properties to its “Select by Hilton” portfolio.

YOTEL operates “highly efficient” hotels in urban markets, filling a customer need in the growing lifestyle segment. The company will continue to independently manage and license its 23 hotels in 10 countries while enabling Hilton (HLT) to expand its portfolio without adding company-owned or franchise properties.

As part of Hilton’s newly established “Select by Hilton” brand, YOTEL can retain its identity while at the same time being connected to the  Hilton Honors loyalty program and its platform of global properties. This would enable YOTEL guests to accumulate Hilton Honors points and enjoy benefits of the Hilton (HLT) brand. 

“YOTEL’s relationship with Hilton allows us to expand our reach while staying true to who we are. What changes for YOTEL is access—not identity—in a capital-light and scalable way,” said YOTEL’s CEO, Phil Andreopoulos.

data.title: DDC Enterprise adds 200 Bitcoin, lifting total holdings to 2.38K BTC
data.description:
• DDC Enterprise (DDC) recently purchased 200 Bitcoin (BTC-USD), increasing its total holdings to 2.38K BTC, while securing the 32nd global rank among public corporate Bitcoin holders.
  • Following this, total holdings now stand at 2.38K BTC at an average cost of ~$79.97K per BTC, with a YTD yield of 44.9% and 0.057626 BTC per 1K DDC shares.
  • The company plans to use BTC as its primary reserve asset and continue buying as part of its Bitcoin accumulation strategy.

data.title: Accenture rises after Q2 beat, revises outlook
data.description:
Shares of Accenture (ACN) rose about 3% on Thursday after fiscal second quarter results beat estimates, despite third quarter revenue coming in below expectations.

The company also raised the lower end of its full fiscal year 2026 revenue and adjusted EPS outlook.

Q2 Metrics 

For the fiscal second quarter ended Feb. 28, revenue rose 8% in U.S. dollar terms (4% in local currency) year-over-year to $18.04B, while GAAP EPS climbed about 4% year-on-year to $2.93. Both top and bottom line numbers surpassed analysts' expectations.

"We delivered record second quarter bookings of $22.1 billion, including a record 41 clients with quarterly bookings greater than $100 million, with revenues at the top of our guided range, while continuing to take significant share in a competitive market," said Accenture's Chair and CEO Julie Sweet.

The company said new bookings for the second quarter of fiscal 2026 were $22.11B, an increase of 6% in U.S. dollars and 1% in local currency compared to the second quarter of fiscal 2025.

The Irish company's Consulting revenues in the first quarter grew 7% (in U.S. dollars) year-over-year to $8.86B, while Managed Services revenues jumped 10% (in U.S. dollars) to $9.18B.

Americas was the top geographic market by revenue, generating $8.90B in the fiscal second quarter, growing 4% (in U.S. dollars) year-over-year.

The company had a free cash flow of $3.7B.

Dividend: Accenture said it has declared a quarterly cash dividend of $1.63 per share for shareholders of record at the close of business on April 9. This dividend, which is payable on May 15, represents a 10% increase over the quarterly dividend rate of $1.48 per share in fiscal 2025.

Stock Buyback: During the second quarter of fiscal 2026, Accenture bought back or redeemed 6.8M shares for a total of $1.7B, including 5.1M shares repurchased in the open market. Accenture’s total remaining share repurchase authority at Feb. 28 was about $4.4B.

Outlook

Accenture said its third-quarter and full-year 2026 business outlook reflect the company’s best view of the potential impact of the conflict in the Middle East in the second half of this fiscal year. It does not take into account a significant escalation or the occurrence of major economic disruption.

The company expects fiscal third quarter revenue to be between $18.35B and $19B (midpoint at $18.675B), versus a consensus revenue estimate of $18.73B. 

The guidance assumes a foreign exchange impact of about +2.5% versus the third quarter of fiscal 2025. It implies 1% to 5% local-currency growth, including about a 1% impact from federal business. Excluding federal, a 2% to 6% local-currency growth.

For the full-year fiscal 2026, Accenture now expects revenue growth (in local currency) to be in the range of 3% to 5%, versus a prior forecast of 2% to 5%. The guidance includes an estimated 1% impact from federal business; excluding federal, 4% to 6% local-currency growth.

The foreign exchange impact on results in USD is expected to be about +2% versus fiscal year 2025. 

The company now expects adjusted EPS to be between $13.65 and $13.90 (midpoint at $13.775) versus a prior forecast of $13.52 and $13.90 (midpoint at $13.71), compared to a consensus EPS estimate of $13.86.

Acquisitions: The company now expects to invest about $5B in acquisitions, with the capacity to do more if opportunities arise.

Accenture raised fiscal 2026 free cash flow guidance by $1B. The company now expects free cash flow to be between $10.8B and $11.5B, versus a prior forecast of $9.8B to $10.5B, reflecting a free cash flow to net income ratio of 1.3.

Full-year 2026 adjusted operating margin is expected to be 15.7% to 15.9%, representing 10 bps to 30 bps expansion over adjusted FY 2025 results.

The company said the fiscal year 2026 operating cash flow expected range is $11.5B to $12.2B.

Fiscal year 2026 property and equipment additions are now expected to be about $700M.

Accenture continues to expect to return at least $9.3B through dividends and share repurchases, an increase of $1B, or 12%, from fiscal year 2025.

Update: The story was updated with additional information on guidance and stock price movement.
